narrative:
Clouds this morning yield to partly sunny skies for the afternoon with high temperatures likely breaking records for today’s date. Showers roll in after midnight and perhaps some thunder. Colder air arrives early Thursday with rain perhaps ending as some wet snow before ending. Dusting-level accumulations are possible at higher elevations, so while it won’t amount to a lot, it is a reminder that it’s still March and major swings in temperature are part of the seasonal weather DNA. Friday will be bright, sunny and seasonable. Weekend weather features milder and dry weather for Saturday, followed by more clouds, somewhat cooler, with perhaps a few showers for Sunday. The roller-coaster ride continues next Monday with a big swing colder.

It's the March Madness edition of the Monday Message, available now for subscribers!

The "Mix and Match" Special Offer continues on all the Snowman O'Mometer Pint Glass and Mug (or you can long for a 5 Golfball Day with that pint glass as well). Buy one, and get another drinkware item at 40% off.

Get the free RWC Mobile App...: Apple and Android

Records To Reset

Last Updated Wed March 11 at 10:46AM by Scott Stephens

Clouds this morning yield to partly sunny skies for the afternoon with high temperatures likely breaking records for today’s date. Showers roll in after midnight and perhaps some thunder. Colder air arrives early Thursday with rain perhaps ending as some wet snow before ending. Dusting-level accumulations are possible at higher elevations, so while it won’t amount to a lot, it is a reminder that it’s still March and major swings in temperature are part of the seasonal weather DNA. Forecast Discussion

Western North Carolina and Southwest Virginia are solidly in a warm-sector air mass today. After morning cloudiness, expect a partly sunny and crazy-warm afternoon with breezy conditions.

A strong cold front will drive a line of showers and maybe an embedded thundershower into the mountains after midnight.

Cold air will be pushing into the mountains at daybreak, so that leftover rain could end as wet snow Thursday morning before rapid clearing commences by afternoon. It will be windy and colder, a harsh deviation from the recent warmth.

Friday features full sunshine and near-normal temperatures. Saturday will be mostly sunny and milder as a silent front slips past.

This front returns northward as a warm front on Sunday, generating more cloudiness, somewhat cooler temperatures, along with a few light showers.

A strong cold front arrives early Monday with additional showers. Rain changes to snow showers Monday afternoon with flurries lingering into Monday night or perhaps even Tuesday as sharply colder air rushes in once again.

Detailed forecast discussion and analysis is only available to RaysWeather supporters.

Monday Message for March 9, 2026

It's March Madness, weather-style, around western North Carolina. We discuss the ups-and-downs of March weather in this week's edition.

Mar 09, 2026
Spring Fever and near-record temperatures

Warm weather is here, at least until the middle of next week. We will be flirting with daily records at a number of locations during this time period.

On Friday, high temperatures will be in record territory for many locations.

On this map, a '1' denotes a forecast high temperature that would surpass a high temperature record for the 6th of March.

Mar 05, 2026
Monday Message for March 2, 2026

It's the first Monday Message for March! We discuss the chilly change for Monday and Tuesday, but the big weather story will be the spring fever that will be breaking out for the second half of the week.
